families.blocker: The only trusted same-schema remote compare lane still leaves native about 14.8x faster than Rust HNSW.
IVF-PQ is already archived as no-go, so it cannot satisfy the final performance-leadership criterion.
DiskANN remains constrained and its benchmark lane is not a trusted native-comparable leadership surface.

summary: The final performance leadership criterion is not met on the current remote x86 authority surface: no core CPU family can honestly claim leadership, and the only trusted same-schema candidate, HNSW, is still blocked by a roughly 14.8x native throughput advantage.
action: Archive the performance-leadership proof as unmet and keep the project out of final completion claims until a new remote authority artifact shows at least one core CPU path beating native on the same recall-gated lane.
